commit.message:
Fix duplicate output detection, %% escaping, and command output forwarding

1. Duplicate output detection: Within-rule duplicates now produce the
   correct error message ("listed multiple times in a command"). Cross-rule
   duplicates only flag outputs owned by different commands. Fixes t2070.

2. %% percent escaping: Use pre-expanded execution path to avoid double
   expansion of %% → % → (empty). Added execute_expanded_rules and
   execute_expanded_rules_parallel methods. Fixes t2071.

3. Command output forwarding: Command stdout/stderr is now shown inline
   (matching C tup behavior), not just on failure.

4. Refactored parallel execution to share wave-based scheduling logic
   via parallel_command_result helper.

Test results: t0000-t2179 now 51/194 passing (up from ~40).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recommendation cycling after catalogue is fully exhausted

Once recommended_story_ids covers the whole catalogue, the previous 2-pass
strategy pipeline always returned the same 6 stories: _fill_remaining's
absolute last-resort path bypassed exclude_ids and pulled already-recommended
stories back in, making the result deterministic and frozen.

Restructure _pick_fresh into a 3-tier pre-filtered approach:

  Tier 1 — novel:    stories never recommended (progressive coverage)
  Tier 2 — previous: recommended before, but outside the last batch
            (cycles through older stories after exhaustion)
  Tier 3 — last batch: most-recent recommendations
            (fallback for near-empty catalogues)

The catalogue is pre-filtered per tier before being passed to
_run_strategy_pipeline, so _fill_remaining's last-resort path can only
reach stories within that tier.  Each tier request is capped to len(tier)
to prevent within-tier duplication.  A final full-catalogue pass retains
the original repeat behaviour for catalogues smaller than 6 stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: taint analysis improvements — sprintf/write! propagation, sanitizer overwrite, C/C++ patterns

- Fix Rust write!/writeln! regex to handle &mut prefix in Pass 2b and template extractor
- Add generic sanitizer function name recognition (sanitize/clean/escape/encode/etc.)
  for field overwrite taint clearing and Pass 3 sanitizer detection
- Add Pass 3b: field-level sanitizer registration (self.cmd = sanitize() → self.cmd safe)
- Add Check 3b in reachability: sanitized field suppresses detection after sanitizer line
- Expand C/C++ command injection patterns: system(variable), popen(variable)
- Add C/C++ literal tracker configs for constant propagation FP prevention
- Add sprintf literal propagation: sprintf(buf, fmt, literals) → buf is safe

Fix: blend sp_one_day_races into flat-stage specialty signal

PCS sp_sprint is accumulated exclusively on bunch-sprint stage wins,
which systematically undervalues puncher/classics riders who win flat
stages via one-day race points (e.g. Girmay: sp_sprint=1520 vs
sp_one_day=3340). On flat finishes the model now blends
sp_sprint (65%) + sp_one_day_races (35%), correcting a large
structural mis-ranking without displacing pure sprinters at the top.

Effect: Girmay #15 → #10, specialty 0.28 → 0.45.
Trentin correctly rises to #3 (sp_one_day=5783).

Updated docs/RANKING.md and README.md to document the blending logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI failures and replace Codecov with local coverage badge

CI fixes (cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings):
- examples/deploy_tool.rs: inline last literal args in println! format strings
- examples/derive_example.rs: same; add #![allow(dead_code)] to inner module
- examples/mcp_server.rs: inline last literal args in eprintln! format strings
- tests/derive_test.rs: add #![allow(dead_code)] — derive structs are
  command descriptors, not data containers; fields intentionally unused
- src/query/mod.rs: results.len() >= 1 → !results.is_empty()

Doc link fixes (RUSTDOCFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo doc --all-features):
- src/render/mod.rs: Cli::with_renderer → crate::Cli::with_renderer
- src/model/command.rs: Cli::run_async → crate::Cli::run_async
- src/cli/mod.rs: CommandBuilder::async_handler → crate::CommandBuilder::async_handler

Coverage workflow:
- Remove Codecov upload (not used)
- Run tarpaulin --out Json, generate shields.io-style SVG badge via Python
- Commit coverage.svg back to main on every push [skip ci]
- Still enforce 80% threshold (exit 1 if below)

Add coverage.svg placeholder (shows "n/a" until first CI run updates it)
README: badge now references coverage.svg instead of codecov.io

fix: backfill creatorId for old events + server-side operator detection

- Attendees endpoint now auto-backfills creatorId from first participant for old events
- Also checks db.events as fallback source for creatorId

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add $autoCancel:false to SalesPage list query + improve debug panel

Root cause: PocketBase SDK auto-cancels in-flight requests when a new
request to the same endpoint is made. useRealtime subscription init can
trigger this race, causing the list query to be cancelled and throw an
error that React Query caches as a failure.

Fix: set '$autoCancel': false on the transactions getList call so it is
never auto-cancelled by the SDK.

Debug panel updated to test [A] simple, [B] perPage20, and [C] show the
exact useQuery error status, message and response data.

commit.message:
feat: implement canopy/accessible-html package

Type-enforced accessibility for Canopy HTML. A drop-in replacement for
the Html module that makes common accessibility violations impossible
at compile time:

- Images require alt text (img takes String argument)
- Inputs require labels (labelBefore, labelAfter, labelHidden wrappers)
- ARIA roles are a closed union type (no typos, full autocompletion)
- Non-interactive elements use Attribute Never to reject event handlers
- Screen-reader-only content via Accessible.Style.invisible
- Keyboard event helpers via Accessible.Key
- Focus management via Accessible.Focus
- Live region attributes via Accessible.Live
- Landmark roles via Accessible.Landmark

321 tests, 97% coverage.

commit.message:
Add ci_post_clone.sh to fix Xcode Cloud builds

Secrets.swift is gitignored (contains API keys) so Xcode Cloud
can't find it after cloning. This post-clone script generates a
placeholder Secrets.swift so the archive build compiles. Set the
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable in Xcode Cloud to inject
the real key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix broken Trap/G-Funk tracks and add Freestyle mode to H3

Bug fix: NOTE_FREQS array was too small (60 entries), causing
out-of-bounds access for Nested Too Deep (key=7) and Justify
My Content (key=5) when computing octave 4 lead notes. Extended
to 84 entries and added bounds clamping in getNote().

New feature: Freestyle mode lets users paste HTML and watch it
play line-by-line with tags lighting up and producing musical
tones layered over any backing beat. Includes 4 sample snippets,
beat picker, speed control, and looping playback.

commit.message:
PhantomBuster: confirm delivery before advancing sequence step

Launch is async — phantom runs in the background. Instead of marking
stepExecuted immediately (same bug we fixed for Instantly), now:

1. Launch phantom, store containerId in enrollment metadata
2. Set nextActionAt to 2 minutes (phantom takes ~30-60s)
3. Next tick: poll GET /agents/fetch for lastEndStatus
4. Only advance step when status === "success"
5. On failure: clear tracking, retry in 1 hour
6. Still running: skip, check again next tick

Prevents advancing sequence steps without confirmed LinkedIn delivery.

feat: Claude Code stdout adapter, standby agent architecture, remove warm pool

- Add extensible StdoutAdapter system with ClaudeCodeAdapter that parses
  ⏺/⎿ markers and ANSI color state to extract only prose text
- Replace warm agent pool (~200 lines) with simple spawn_standby_agent
  that reuses the normal spawn path with "Wait for network activity"

  spawn one. Always exactly one standby after first message.

  router/spawn when a standby is already listening

commit.message:
Images: srcset, onerror fallback, eager first cards, width/height attrs

- wikimediaSrcset(): auto-generates 480w/960w srcset from Wikimedia thumb
  URLs by swapping the width token in the path (e.g. 960px- → 480px-)
- cardImgHTML(): now includes Wikimedia srcset (or imgix when configured),
  width="960" height="540", and onerror="onImgError(this)"
- onImgError(): shared handler — removes broken img, adds .img-failed to
  the wrap so the sized box is preserved (no CLS on error)
- enhanceStaticImages(container): walks all img.card-img after DOM load or
  fetchSection, adding width/height, onerror, and srcset to static HTML images
- setEagerImages(): promotes the first 2 imgs in the active weekend panel
  to loading="eager" so they load immediately on page open
- fetchSection(): calls enhanceStaticImages(main) after injecting HTML
- CSS: .card-img-wrap gets position:relative; .img-failed shows a subtle
  📷 fallback icon centred in the preserved 16:9 box; placeholder bg
  updated to #e5e7eb (standard neutral grey)
- highlight row img: add width/height/decoding/onerror
